allow:
Bash(mkdir -p home && git mv \\
  .chezmoi.toml.tmpl \\
  .chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl \\
  .chezmoiignore \\
  .gitpod.yml \\
  AppData \\
  dot_backup \\
  dot_bashrc.tmpl \\
  dot_config \\
  dot_continue \\
  dot_gitconfig.tmpl \\
  dot_hammerspoon \\
  dot_hushlogin \\
  dot_liquidpromptrc.tmpl \\
  dot_nanorc.tmpl \\
  dot_profile \\
  dot_zsh_custom \\
  dot_zshrc.tmpl \\
  encrypted_dot_pkmn.gitconfig.age \\
  encrypted_dot_pkmn.zshrc.age \\
  private_dot_claude \\
  private_dot_gnupg \\
  private_dot_keys \\
  private_dot_ssh \\
  private_Library \\
  run_once_before_decrypt-private-key.sh.tmpl \\
  run_onchange_cleanup-packages.sh.tmpl \\
  run_onchange_install-packages.ps1 \\
  run_onchange_install-packages.sh.tmpl \\
  run_upgrade.sh.tmpl \\
  Documents \\
  home/)

Bash(ls home/.* 2>/dev/null || echo "No hidden files visible"
ls -la home/ | head -30)
Bash(command -v mise && mise list 2>/dev/null | head -20 || echo "mise not in PATH")

Bash(git -C /Users/vlad/.local/share/chezmoi add home/.chezmoi.toml.tmpl && git -C /Users/vlad/.local/share/chezmoi commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Fix chezmoi.toml.tmpl: double ??, p/p ambiguity, fastfetch_colors unavailable

- Remove trailing '?' from all promptChoiceOnce/promptMultichoiceOnce strings
  \(chezmoi appends its own '?' — having one in the string caused double '??'\)
- Change identity option 'personal' → 'home' to avoid prefix ambiguity with
  'pkmn' \(both started with 'p'; chezmoi uses prefix matching so 'p' was
  ambiguous\). $pokemon check unchanged: still eq $identity "pkmn"
- Replace .fastfetch_colors lookup with inline if/else chain
  \(.chezmoidata/ is loaded after .chezmoi.toml.tmpl renders, so the map
  wasn't available — caused 'map has no entry for key fastfetch_colors'\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C /Users/vlad/.local/share/chezmoi rm home/.chezmoidata/packages/core.toml home/.chezmoidata/packages/dev.toml home/.chezmoidata/packages/cloud.toml home/.chezmoidata/packages/security.toml home/.chezmoidata/packages/ai.toml home/.chezmoidata/packages/pkmn.toml home/.chezmoidata/packages/apps.toml home/.chezmoidata/packages/gaming.toml home/.chezmoidata/packages/creative.toml home/.chezmoidata/packages/comms.toml)

Bash(git -C /Users/vlad/.local/share/chezmoi add home/.chezmoidata/packages.toml && git -C /Users/vlad/.local/share/chezmoi commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Fix packages data: consolidate from subdirectory into single packages.toml

chezmoi does not recurse into .chezmoidata/ subdirectories — only top-level
files are read. The per-group files in packages/ were never loaded, causing
'map has no entry for key packages' in install templates.

Consolidate all groups into home/.chezmoidata/packages.toml using nested
TOML sections \([packages.core], [packages.dev], etc.\) so .packages.* is
available in all templates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git -C /Users/vlad/.local/share/chezmoi add -A && git -C /Users/vlad/.local/share/chezmoi commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Fix template map access and add check.sh test harness

Template fixes \(all caused by Go template map access rules\):
- index $data "required" / index $gdata "description": dot-notation on maps
  panics for missing keys; index returns nil instead
- "A | default dict | index key": pipeline passes value as LAST arg, making
  "key" the collection — fix with index \(A | default dict\) "key"
- $.disabled_packages / .extra_packages: machine-local keys absent on fresh
  installs — fix with index $ "key" | default list/nil

Add check.sh at repo root:
- Runs chezmoi execute-template on every .tmpl file
- Reports pass/fail per template with error messages
- Skips .chezmoi.toml.tmpl \(uses config-only promptChoiceOnce\)
- Run before chezmoi apply: ./check.sh

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(/Users/vlad/.local/share/chezmoi/check.sh && git -C /Users/vlad/.local/share/chezmoi add home/run_onchange_before_10-install-darwin.sh.tmpl home/Brewfile.tmpl && git -C /Users/vlad/.local/share/chezmoi commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
Use brew install --cask --adopt to handle pre-existing apps

Without --adopt, Homebrew errors if an app already exists at the install
path \(e.g. Steam installed directly before Homebrew managed it\).
--adopt takes ownership of the existing installation without reinstalling,
making the install idempotent on both fresh and existing machines.

Also note --adopt in Brewfile comment for brew bundle usage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
